assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised monetary item between group entities with different functional currencies.
The identified foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is the most direct hedge accounting model for a recognised foreign-currency intragroup monetary receivable whose exchange differences affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is also relevant because IFRS 9 permits recognised assets or liabilities to be designated in a cash flow hedge when risk-driven cash flow variability could affect profit or loss.
